title: CI flaky: test_linearize_jump_load_with_mask fails in integration-tests-ascend
body: ## Summary`integration-tests-ascend` is intermittently failing on the existing upstream test:```textthird_party/ascend/unittest/pytest_ut/test_linearize.py::test_linearize_jump_load_with_mask[5-15-dtype0-float32]```This is currently blocking several tutorial-only PRs whose diffs do not touch `third_party/ascend/unittest/pytest_ut/test_linearize.py` or the compiler/runtime code.## Observed failuresRecent PR check failures all report the same test, with different mismatch values/indices across runs:- #686 py3.11: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/actions/runs/27863703794/job/82464045572- #687 py3.10: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/actions/runs/27863705307/job/82464048341- #688 py3.10: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/actions/runs/27863707308/job/82464057135- #688 py3.11: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/actions/runs/27863707308/job/82464057142- #689 py3.11: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/actions/runs/27863708530/job/82464059056Example failure excerpt:```textAssertionError: Tensor-likes are not close!Mismatched elements: 8 / 75 (10.7%)Greatest absolute difference: 10.63780689239502 at index (1, 0, 13) (up to 0.0001 allowed)Greatest relative difference: 1.0 at index (1, 0, 3) (up to 0.0001 allowed)```Other runs of the same test reported `22 / 75` mismatches and different max-diff locations.## Why this looks unrelated to the tutorial PRsThe failing file is already present on `upstream/main` and appears to have been added by:```text31a5d8ca15c569df62cb87142ea3d037f0a9ef32[driver](fix) Fix get_active_torch_device NameError in Ascend driver (#490)```The affected tutorial PRs only modify their own directories under `third_party/ascend/tutorials/<op>/`.## Suspected failing patternThe failing test calls `save_cache_to_buffer_with_mask` in `test_linearize.py`. The suspicious path is the odd-`pid_loc` branch with masked `tl.load`:```pythonif pid_loc % 2 == 1:    tmp = tl.load(        cache_ptr2 + (2 * BLOCK * cache_index_0 + cache_index_1),        (buffer_index < MASK_NUM) & (buffer_index < mask_int),    )    tl.store(buffer_ptr + index, tmp)```For the failing parameter set `batch_size=5, buffer_len=15`, `pid_loc=1` has `mask_int=2`, so only the first two elements should be valid and the rest should remain zero in the reference path. The CI failure shows mismatches in the tail positions, suggesting a possible issue in masked load/linearization/codegen or a flaky runtime interaction for this pattern.## Local checkUsing a local OpForge environment with CANN 9.0.0, the specific test did not reproduce:```bashASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7 python -m pytest -q \  'third_party/ascend/unittest/pytest_ut/test_linearize.py::test_linearize_jump_load_with_mask[5-15-dtype0-float32]'```This passed repeatedly locally, while the hosted CI failed in both py3.10 and py3.11 jobs. The hosted CI container uses its own CANN/Python/build environment, so this may be environment or build-artifact sensitive.## RequestCould maintainers please investigate this test or mark it flaky/skip it until stable? It is currently blocking unrelated tutorial-only PRs through the required integration checks.

title: [Feature]: enable autoblockify before ssbuffer pass
body: ### 🚀 The feature, motivation and pitch对于一些算子，其计算逻辑本身缺少for循环，于是无法进行ssbuffer的流水排布。但是autoblockify pass会将过大的逻辑线程数量分配到实际物理核上，即会在算子主体计算逻辑之外添加一层for循环，将多个逻辑核线程放在一个物理核线程上计算。因此，ssbuffer可以基于这一层额外的for循环上进行流水排布，但是autoblockify pass在npuir上，而ssbuffer相关pass在TA的最后一层，需要将底层的autoblockify pass迁移到上层来完成。### Alternatives_No response_### Additional context_No response_

title: [Misc]:  autotune 中 BLOCK_SIZE=2048 的 config 未被选中/被剪枝，缺乏可见性
body: ### Anything you want to discuss about triton on ascend.背景我在进行的 autotune 实践时，基于 autotune.py 样例代码（size=98432，原始 get_autotune_config() 返回  BLOCK_SIZE=16/256/512 三个 config），新增了 triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 1024}) 和 triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 2048}) 两个配置。运行环境为 Ascend  910B，Triton-ascend 镜像版本 swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/hw-ascend/triton:3.2.1-910b-openeuler24.03-py3.11-arm64，torch-npu 2.7.1，已设置  TRITON_PRINT_AUTOTUNING=1。  复现步骤  # autotune.py 中修改 get_autotune_config()  def get_autotune_config():      return [          triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 16}),          triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 256}),          triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 512}),          triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 1024}), # ← 加了这行          triton.Config({'BLOCK_SIZE': 2048}),   # ← 加了这行      ]  # 启用 autotune 调试输出  export TRITON_PRINT_AUTOTUNING=1  # 运行  python3 autotune.py  实际现象  - BLOCK_SIZE=16, 256, 512, 1024 四个 config 均被 benchmark 并参与比较  - BLOCK_SIZE=1024 最终被选中  - BLOCK_SIZE=2048 完全没有出现在 autotune 输出中 — 既没有被 benchmark，也没有被报告为失败/跳过  预期行为  BLOCK_SIZE=2048 应该至少被尝试 benchmark，然后根据性能数据决定是否选中。如果被剪枝，应有日志说明原因。

title: triton-ascend autotune [] 报错No valid triton configs. NoneType: None
body:
### Describe the bugimport torchimport tritonimport triton.language as tlimport triton.language.extra.cann.extension as extensionimport triton.backends.ascend.runtimeDEVICE = (    "cuda"    if torch.cuda.is_available()    else "npu"    if torch.npu.is_available()    else "cpu")@triton.jitdef addmm_kernel_no_autotune(    y_ptr,    x1_ptr,    x2_ptr,    bias_ptr,    M: tl.constexpr,    N: tl.constexpr,    K: tl.constexpr,    alpha,    beta,    stride_x1_m,    stride_x1_k,    stride_x2_k,    stride_x2_n,    stride_y_m,    stride_y_n,    stride_bias_m,    stride_bias_n,    BLOCK_M: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,):    total_pid = tl.num_programs(0)    current_pid = tl.program_id(0)    total_m_blocks = tl.cdiv(M, BLOCK_M)    total_n_blocks = tl.cdiv(N, BLOCK_N)    # process BLOCK_M * N    for pid_m in range(current_pid, total_m_blocks, total_pid):        m_indices = pid_m * BLOCK_M + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_M)        mask_m = m_indices < M        acc = tl.zeros((BLOCK_M, N), dtype=tl.float32)        # K block loop        for k in range(0, K, BLOCK_K):            k_indices = k + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)            mask_k = k_indices < K            # load X1[BLOCK_M, BLOCK_K]            x1 = tl.load(                x1_ptr                + m_indices[:, None] * stride_x1_m                + k_indices[None, :] * stride_x1_k,                mask=mask_m[:, None] & mask_k[None, :],                other=0.0,            )            # N block loop, reuse x1            for pid_n in range(total_n_blocks):                n_start = pid_n * BLOCK_N                n_indices = n_start + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)                mask_n = n_indices < N                x2 = tl.load(                    x2_ptr                    + k_indices[:, None] * stride_x2_k                    + n_indices[None, :] * stride_x2_n,                    mask=mask_k[:, None] & mask_n[None, :],                    other=0.0,                )                d = tl.dot(x1, x2)                acc_n = extension.extract_slice(                    acc, (0, n_start), (BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), (1, 1)                )                acc_n = acc_n + d                acc = extension.insert_slice(                    acc, acc_n, (0, n_start), (BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), (1, 1)                )        # store result        for pid_n in range(total_n_blocks):            n_start = pid_n * BLOCK_N            n_indices = n_start + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)            mask_n = n_indices < N            mask_out = mask_m[:, None] & mask_n[None, :]            y_block = extension.extract_slice(                acc, (0, n_start), (BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), (1, 1)            )            bias = tl.load(                bias_ptr                + m_indices[:, None] * stride_bias_m                + n_indices[None, :] * stride_bias_n,                mask=mask_out,                other=0.0,            )            y = alpha * y_block + beta * bias            acc = extension.insert_slice(                acc, y, (0, n_start), (BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), (1, 1)            )        tl.store(            y_ptr            + m_indices[:, None] * stride_y_m            + tl.arange(0, N)[None, :] * stride_y_n,            acc,            mask=mask_m[:, None],        )@triton.autotune(    configs=[],    key=["M", "N", "K"],)@triton.jitdef addmm_kernel_autotune(    y_ptr,    x1_ptr,    x2_ptr,    bias_ptr,    M,    N,    K,    alpha,    beta,    stride_x1_m,    stride_x1_k,    stride_x2_k,    stride_x2_n,    stride_y_m,    stride_y_n,    stride_bias_m,    stride_bias_n,    BLOCK_M: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,):    total_pid = tl.num_programs(0)  # AI Core number    current_pid = tl.program_id(0)  # core ID    # M & N dim split to (m_block, n_block)    total_m_blocks = tl.cdiv(M, BLOCK_M)    total_n_blocks = tl.cdiv(N, BLOCK_N)    total_output_blocks = total_m_blocks * total_n_blocks    for block_idx in range(current_pid, total_output_blocks, total_pid):        # block_idx convert to 2D (pid_m, pid_n)        pid_m = block_idx // total_n_blocks        pid_n = block_idx % total_n_blocks        offs_m = pid_m * BLOCK_M + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_M)        offs_n = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)        offs_k = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)        mask_m = offs_m < M        mask_n = offs_n < N        mask_out = mask_m[:, None] & mask_n[None, :]        acc = tl.zeros((BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N), dtype=tl.float32)        # K block loop        for k in range(0, K, BLOCK_K):            current_k = k + offs_k            mask_k = current_k < K            # load x1 block [BLOCK_M, BLOCK_K]            x1 = tl.load(                x1_ptr                + offs_m[:, None] * stride_x1_m                + current_k[None, :] * stride_x1_k,                mask=mask_m[:, None] & mask_k[None, :],                other=0.0,            )            # load x2 block [BLOCK_K, BLOCK_N]            x2 = tl.load(                x2_ptr                + current_k[:, None] * stride_x2_k                + offs_n[None, :] * stride_x2_n,                mask=mask_k[:, None] & mask_n[None, :],                other=0.0,            )            acc += tl.dot(x1, x2)        bias = tl.load(            bias_ptr            + offs_m[:, None] * stride_bias_m            + offs_n[None, :] * stride_bias_n,            mask=mask_out,            other=0.0,        )        y = alpha * acc + beta * bias        tl.store(            y_ptr + offs_m[:, None] * stride_y_m + offs_n[None, :] * stride_y_n,            y,            mask=mask_out,        )def triton_addmm(    x1: torch.Tensor,    x2: torch.Tensor,    bias: torch.Tensor,    alpha: float = 1.0,    beta: float = 1.0,    BLOCK_M=None,    BLOCK_N=None,    BLOCK_K=None,) -> torch.Tensor:    x1 = x1.to(DEVICE)    x2 = x2.to(DEVICE)    bias = bias.to(DEVICE)    if x1.dim() != 2 or x2.dim() != 2 or bias.dim() != 2:        raise ValueError("x1, x2, bias 必须是2维张量")    M, K = x1.shape    K2, N = x2.shape    if K != K2:        raise ValueError(f"x1的K={K}与x2的K={K2}不匹配")    if bias.shape != (M, N):        raise ValueError(f"bias shape 必须为 ({M}, {N})")    y = torch.empty((M, N), device=x1.device, dtype=x1.dtype)    if DEVICE == "npu":        import triton.runtime.driver as driver        device = torch.npu.current_device()        aicore_num = driver.active.utils.get_device_properties(device)["num_aicore"]        grid = lambda meta: (            M_num := triton.cdiv(M, meta["BLOCK_M"]),            N_num := triton.cdiv(                N,                meta["BLOCK_N"],            ),            (N_num, M_num) if M_num * N_num < aicore_num / 2 else (aicore_num,),        )[-1]    else:        grid = lambda meta: (            triton.cdiv(N, meta["BLOCK_N"], triton.cdiv(M, meta["BLOCK_M"]))        )    USE_AUTOTUNE = BLOCK_M is None or BLOCK_N is None or BLOCK_K is None    if USE_AUTOTUNE:        addmm_kernel_autotune[grid](            y_ptr=y,            x1_ptr=x1,            x2_ptr=x2,            bias_ptr=bias,            M=M,            N=N,            K=K,            alpha=alpha,            beta=beta,            stride_x1_m=x1.stride(0),            stride_x1_k=x1.stride(1),            stride_x2_k=x2.stride(0),            stride_x2_n=x2.stride(1),            stride_y_m=y.stride(0),            stride_y_n=y.stride(1),            stride_bias_m=bias.stride(0),            stride_bias_n=bias.stride(1),        )    else:        addmm_kernel_no_autotune[grid](            y_ptr=y,            x1_ptr=x1,            x2_ptr=x2,            bias_ptr=bias,            M=M,            N=N,            K=K,            alpha=alpha,            beta=beta,            stride_x1_m=x1.stride(0),            stride_x1_k=x1.stride(1),            stride_x2_k=x2.stride(0),            stride_x2_n=x2.stride(1),            stride_y_m=y.stride(0),            stride_y_n=y.stride(1),            stride_bias_m=bias.stride(0),            stride_bias_n=bias.stride(1),            BLOCK_M=BLOCK_M,            BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,            BLOCK_K=BLOCK_K,        )    return y测试代码：import 1.001]],    torch.float8_e4m3fn: [[0.001, -0.999], [-0.999, 1.001]],    torch.float8_e5m2: [[0.001, -0.999], [-0.999, 1.001]],}DTYPES = [torch.float32, torch.float16, torch.float8_e4m3fn, torch.float8_e5m2]SEEDS = [3]# 精度标准RTOL = {    torch.float32: 1e-5,    torch.float16: 1e-3,    torch.float8_e4m3fn: 1e-3,    torch.float8_e5m2: 1e-3,}ATOL = {    torch.float32: 1e-5,    torch.float16: 1e-3,    torch.float8_e4m3fn: 1e-3,    torch.float8_e5m2: 1e-3,}# 性能测试标准PERFORMANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.9  # 0.9 * AscendCWARMUP = 0ITER = 1def torch_addmm(    x1: torch.Tensor, x2: torch.Tensor, bias: torch.Tensor, alpha: float, beta: float):    """torch 实现"""    return torch.addmm(bias, x1, x2, beta=beta, alpha=alpha)def run_example(    M: int,    N: int,    K: int,    alpha: float,    beta: float,    DTYPE: torch.dtype,    seed: int = 123,):    torch.manual_seed(seed)    # 构造数据    x1 = torch.randn(M, K, dtype=torch.float32).to(DTYPE)    x2 = torch.randn(K, N, dtype=torch.float32).to(DTYPE)    bias = torch.randn(M, N, dtype=torch.float32).to(DTYPE)    # 运行    y_torch = torch_addmm(x1, x2, bias, alpha, beta).to(torch.float32)    y_triton = triton_addmm(x1, x2, bias, alpha, beta).cpu().to(torch.float32)    # 校验    ok = torch.allclose(y_triton, y_torch, rtol=RTOL[DTYPE], atol=ATOL[DTYPE])    max_err = torch.max(torch.abs(y_triton - y_torch)).item()    print(        f"📌 M={M:4d} N={N:4d} K={K:4d} | dtype={DTYPE} | alpha={alpha:.4f} beta={beta:.4f}"    )    print(f"   精度：{'✅ PASS' if ok else '❌ FAIL'} | 最大误差: {max_err:.6f}
")# ====================== 函数3：pytest 精度测试 ======================test_cases = []for dtype in DTYPES:    # 每个 dtype 取自己对应的 scalar list    scalars = SCALARS[dtype]    # 组合：M × N × K × dtype × scalar    cases_for_dtype = list(        itertools.product(M_LIST, N_LIST, K_LIST, scalars, [dtype], SEEDS)    )    test_cases.extend(cases_for_dtype)@pytest.mark.parametrize("M, N, K, scalar, DTYPE, SEED", test_cases)def test_accuracy_triton_addmm(M, N, K, scalar, DTYPE, SEED):    alpha = scalar[0]    beta = scalar[1]    run_example(M, N, K, alpha, beta, DTYPE, SEED)configs = []for m, n, k in [[495, 5333, 1536], [64, 512, 128], [16, 64, 24]]:    for block_m, block_n in [        [32, 32],        [64, 64],        [128, 128],        [192, 192],        [32, 64],        [64, 128],        [128, 192],    ]:        configs.append(            triton.testing.Benchmark(                # argument names to use as an x-axis for the plot                x_names=[                    "BLOCK_K",                ],                x_vals=[                    32,                    64,                    96,                    128,                    256,                    512,                ],  # different possible values for `x_name`                line_arg="provider",                # argument name whose value corresponds to a different line in the plot                # possible values for `line_arg``                line_vals=["torch", "triton"],                # label name for the lines                line_names=["torch", "triton"],                # line styles                styles=[("green", "-"), ("blue", "-")],                ylabel="runtime(ms)",  # label name for the y-axis                plot_name=f"addmm-performance-{m}-{n}-{k}-{block_m}-{block_n}",                # name for the plot. Used also as a file name for saving the plot.                args={                    "M": m,                    "N": n,                    "K": k,                    "BLOCK_M": block_m,                    "BLOCK_N": block_n,                },            )        )def benchmark_perf(M, N, K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K, provider):    DTYPE = torch.float16    alpha, beta = 1.218, 1.0  # 1.0会调用matmul, 非1.0会调用vector的muls    x1 = torch.randn(M, K, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    x2 = torch.randn(K, N, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    bias = torch.randn(M, N, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    USE_AUTOTUNE = BLOCK_M == 0 or BLOCK_N == 0 or BLOCK_K == 0    if provider == "torch":        ms = triton.testing.do_bench(lambda: torch_addmm(x1, x2, bias, alpha, beta))    if provider == "triton":        if USE_AUTOTUNE:            ms = triton.testing.do_bench(                lambda: triton_addmm(x1, x2, bias, alpha, beta)            )        else:            ms = triton.testing.do_bench(                lambda: triton_addmm(                    x1, x2, bias, alpha, beta, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K                )            )    return ms@triton.testing.perf_report(configs)def do_bench_perf(M, N, K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K, provider):    return benchmark_perf(M, N, K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K, provider)def run_perf_testcase(caseId, perfMod, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K):    """性能测试函数"""    """caseId: 0: large, 1: middle, 2: small"""    """perfMode: bit0: run triton, bit1: run torch """    assert (        caseId == 0 or caseId == 1 or caseId == 2    ), "Error input caseId={}, expect in [0, 1, 2]".format(caseId)    # 性能测试使用最大矩阵，最能体现性能    MKN_LIST = [[495, 5333, 1536], [64, 512, 128], [16, 64, 24]]    M, K, N = MKN_LIST[caseId]  # caseId: 0: large, 1: middle, 2: small    DTYPE = torch.float16    print("
" + "=" * 60)    print(" Triton Addmm 性能测试 caseId={} M={} N={} K={}".format(caseId, M, N, K))    print("=" * 60)    """perfMode: bit0: run triton, bit1: run torch"""    # 构造输入    x1 = torch.randn(M, K, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    x2 = torch.randn(K, N, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    bias = torch.randn(M, N, dtype=DTYPE).to(DEVICE)    # 测速    t_torch = (        benchmark_perf(M, N, K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K, "torch")        if perfMod > 1        else 0    )    t_triton = (        benchmark_perf(M, N, K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K, "triton")        if perfMod % 2        else 1e-12    )    # 性能倍率    ratio = t_torch / t_triton    meet_standard = ratio >= PERFORMANCE_THRESHOLD    # 输出    print(        f"[矩阵尺寸] M={M}, N={N}, K={K}, BLOCK_M={BLOCK_M}, BLOCK_N={BLOCK_N}, BLOCK_K={BLOCK_K}"    )    print(f"[数据类型] {dtype}")    print(f"[PyTorch]  平均耗时: {t_torch:>.3f} ms")    print(f"[Triton]   平均耗时: {t_triton:>.3f} ms")    print(f"[性能倍率] {ratio:.3f}x")    print(f"[达标 0.9×AscendC] : {'✅ 满足' if meet_standard else '❌ 不满足'}")    print("=" * 60 + "
")if __name__ == "__main__":    print(        "======== start time={} ========".format(            datetime.now(timezone(timedelta(hours=8)))        )    )    # 不输入参数, do_bench跑指定切块的性能    if len(sys.argv) == 1:        do_bench_perf.run(show_plots=True, print_data=True, save_path=".")    # 运行精度用例    if len(sys.argv) == 2:        isRunAccu = len(sys.argv) == 2 and sys.argv[1] != 0        if isRunAccu:            for M, N, K, scalar, DTYPE, SEED in test_cases:                alpha = scalar[0]                beta = scalar[1]                run_example(M, N, K, alpha, beta, DTYPE, SEED)    # 运行autotune    if len(sys.argv) == 4:        M = int(sys.argv[1])        N = int(sys.argv[2])        K = int(sys.argv[3])        ts = benchmark_perf(M, N, K, 0, 0, 0, "triton")        # ==========================        # 🔥 打印最终选择 + 性能        # ==========================        best_conf = addmm_kernel_autotune.best_config        best_time = addmm_kernel_autotune.bench_time        tflops = 2 * M * N * K / (best_time * 1e-3) / 1e12        print("
==================================================")        print("           🚀 AUTO TUNE 最终结果                 ")        print("==================================================")        print(f"Shape: M={M}, N={N}, K={K}")        print(f"BLOCK_M    = {best_conf.kwargs['BLOCK_M']}")        print(f"BLOCK_N    = {best_conf.kwargs['BLOCK_N']}")        print(f"BLOCK_K    = {best_conf.kwargs['BLOCK_K']}")        print(f"num_warps  = {best_conf.num_warps}")        print(f"num_stages = {best_conf.num_stages}")        print(f"Best time  = {best_time:.4f} ms")        print(f"TFLOPS     = {tflops:.4f}")        print("==================================================
")    # 指定BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K,运行性能测试和Profiling    if len(sys.argv) == 6:        caseId = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) >= 2 else 0        perfMod = int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv) >= 3 else 3        BLOCK_M = int(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) >= 4 else 128        BLOCK_N = int(sys.argv[4]) if len(sys.argv) >= 5 else 128        BLOCK_K = int(sys.argv[5]) if len(sys.argv) >= 6 else 128        run_perf_testcase(caseId, perfMod, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, BLOCK_K)    print(        "======== end time={} ========".format(            datetime.now(timezone(timedelta(hours=8)))        ) sparse_indices.cpu().numpy()    actual_seq_lengths_query = actual_seq_lengths_query.cpu().numpy()    actual_seq_lengths_kv = actual_seq_lengths_kv.cpu().numpy()    query_rope = query_rope.cpu().detach().to(torch.float32).numpy()    key_rope = key_rope.cpu().detach().to(torch.float32).numpy()    batch_size = actual_seq_lengths_query.shape[0]    num_heads = query.shape[2]    num_kv_heads = key.shape[2]    sparse_count = sparse_indices.shape[-1]    g = num_heads // num_kv_heads # group size    if layout_query == 'TND':        actual_seq_lengths_query = cpu_trans_tnd_actseq(actual_seq_lengths_query)        query = cpu_trans_tnd_to_bsnd(query, query.shape, actual_seq_lengths_query)        query_rope = cpu_trans_tnd_to_bsnd(query_rope, query_rope.shape, actual_seq_lengths_query)        sparse_indices = cpu_trans_tnd_to_bsnd(sparse_indices, sparse_indices.shape, actual_seq_lengths_query)    q_bnsd_tensor = np.transpose(np.concatenate([query, query_rope], axis=-1), axes=(0, 2, 1, 3))    k_bnsd_tensor = np.transpose(np.concatenate([key, key_rope], axis=-1), axes=(0, 2, 1, 3))    v_bnsd_tensor = np.transpose(value, axes=(0, 2, 1, 3))    sparse_indices = np.transpose(sparse_indices, axes=(0, 2, 1, 3))    matmul_dtype = np.float32    out_shape_bnsd = list(q_bnsd_tensor.shape)    out_shape_bnsd[-1] = out_shape_bnsd[-1] - query_rope.shape[-1]    y = np.zeros(out_shape_bnsd, dtype=np.float32)    for batch in range(batch_size):        cur_acutal_seq_lengths_q = actual_seq_lengths_query[batch]        cur_actual_seq_lengths_kv = actual_seq_lengths_kv[batch]        for n2_idx in range(num_kv_heads):            for s1_idx in range(cur_acutal_seq_lengths_q):                q_curr = q_bnsd_tensor[batch, n2_idx * g: (n2_idx + 1) * g, s1_idx, :]                cur_sparse_indices = sparse_indices[batch, n2_idx, s1_idx, :]                k_sparse, v_sparse = gather_kv(k_bnsd_tensor, v_bnsd_tensor, cur_sparse_indices, sparse_block_size,                                            batch, n2_idx, cur_actual_seq_lengths_kv)                                mm1_res = np.matmul(q_curr.astype(np.float32), k_sparse.astype(np.float32).T, dtype=matmul_dtype)                scale_res = mm1_res * scale_value                if sparse_mode == 3:                    mask_res = mask(scale_res, cur_acutal_seq_lengths_q, cur_actual_seq_lengths_kv,                                    cur_sparse_indices, s1_idx, sparse_block_size)                else:                    mask_res = scale_res                softmax_res = softmax(mask_res)                mm2_res = np.matmul(softmax_res, v_sparse, dtype=matmul_dtype)                y[batch, n2_idx * g: (n2_idx + 1) * g, s1_idx, :] = mm2_res    if layout_query == 'TND':        cpu_out = torch.tensor(y)        return cpu_trans_bnsd_to_tnd(cpu_out, cpu_out.shape, actual_seq_lengths_query)    return torch.from_numpy(np.transpose(y, axes=(0, 2, 1, 3)))def test_op(T, B, KV_S, Q_N, KV_N, D, D_rope, sparse_size, scale_value, sparse_block_size, sparse_mode, block_size, act_kv_s):        assert sparse_size <= act_kv_s    assert KV_N == 1    assert sparse_mode == 0 or 3    assert sparse_block_size == 1    assert (B * KV_S) % block_size == 0    assert D == 512    assert D_rope == 0 or 64    assert act_kv_s % block_size == 0    assert sparse_size % 32 == 0    assert Q_N % 16 == 0        qkv_dtype = torch.float16    DEVICE = "npu"        query = torch.empty((T, Q_N, D), dtype=qkv_dtype, device=DEVICE).normal_(mean=0.0, std=0.5).requires_grad_()    key = torch.empty((B * KV_S // block_size, block_size, KV_N, D), dtype=qkv_dtype, device=DEVICE).normal_(mean=0.0, std=0.5).requires_grad_()    value = key.clone()    act_q_s = T // B # step    rand_vals = torch.rand(T, KV_N, act_kv_s, device=DEVICE)    _, indices = torch.topk(rand_vals, sparse_size, dim=-1)    sparse_indices = indices.to(torch.int32)        actual_seq_lengths_query = torch.tensor([1]).to(torch.int32).to("npu")    actual_seq_lengths_kv = torch.tensor([act_kv_s] * B, dtype=torch.int32, device=DEVICE)    block_table = torch.tensor([range(B * KV_S // block_size)], dtype=torch.int32, device=DEVICE).reshape(B, -1)    if D_rope == 0:        query_rope = None        key_rope = None    else:        query_rope = torch.empty((T, Q_N, D_rope), dtype=qkv_dtype, device=DEVICE).normal_(mean=0.0, std=0.5).requires_grad_()        key_rope = torch.empty((B * KV_S // block_size, block_size, KV_N, D_rope), dtype=qkv_dtype, device=DEVICE).normal_(mean=0.0, std=0.5).requires_grad_()        common_args = {        "query": query,        "key": key,        "value": value,        "sparse_indices": sparse_indices,        "scale_value": scale_value,        "sparse_block_size": sparse_block_size,        "actual_seq_lengths_query": actual_seq_lengths_query,        "actual_seq_lengths_kv": actual_seq_lengths_kv,        "query_rope": query_rope,        "key_rope": key_rope,        "layout_query": 'TND',        "layout_kv": 'PA_BSND',        "sparse_mode": sparse_mode,        "block_table": block_table,    }    tri_out = triton_sparse_flash_attention(**common_args)        cpu_out = cpu_sparse_flash_attention(**common_args)        cpu_out = cpu_out.to(tri_out.dtype).to("npu")    print("tri_out", tri_out, tri_out.shape)    print("cpu_out", cpu_out, cpu_out.shape)        time_cost = do_bench_npu(lambda: triton_sparse_flash_attention(**common_args), warmup=1, active=5)    print(f"Timing Cost: {time_cost}")    torch.testing.assert_close(tri_out, cpu_out, atol=1e-3, rtol=1e-3, equal_nan=True)    print(f"[PASSED]")if __name__ == "__main__":    test_op(T=1, B=1, KV_S=48000, Q_N=128, KV_N=1, D=512, D_rope=64, sparse_size=2048, scale_value=0.5, sparse_block_size=1, sparse_mode=3, block_size=128, title: [Performance] Optimize structured strided memory access on Ascend 910_95
body: ## ProblemOn Ascend 910_95 SIMT kernels, structured strided `tl.load` / `tl.store` accesses are lowered through the legacy structured path:```texttl.load / tl.store  -> reinterpret_cast(offset, size, stride)  -> memref.load / memref.copy```This path is still suitable for contiguous accesses and static power-of-two strides, which can be handled by the existing strided DMA / deinterleave path.However, for 1D tensors and non-permuted block pointers whose last-axis stride is static non-power-of-two or dynamic, the legacy structured path can become inefficient. These cases should be dispatched to the SIMT indirect load/store path on 910_95.There is a second, related performance issue in FLA-style kernels. Some kernels either read a column with an H-axis split stride, or launch many tiny contiguous tiles. Both shapes create small or inefficient DMAs even though the accessed datacan be made contiguous by safely coalescing work inside one program.## Expected BehaviorOn 91095 with SIMT template enabled:```textstructured load/store  |- permuted access                  -> existing ImplicitPermute path  |- static power-of-two last stride  -> legacy reinterpret_cast / strided DMA handled by compact mode  `- dynamic or static non-pow2 stride -> indirect_load / indirect_store```When a safe coalescing pattern is found, the adapter should also reduce the launch grid in Triton-Ascend itself.

body: Consider cases as below:1. this custom op will return tensor<!tt.ptr> value, it will enter in OffsetAnalysis```%a_ptrs_83 = hivm.hir.custom {SrcPtrIndex = array<i32: 0>, hivm.is_distributed, hivm.pipe = #hivm.pipe<PIPE_S>, hivm.tcore_type = #hivm.tcore_type<CUBE_AND_VECTOR>, hivm.vf_mode = #hivm.vf_mode<SIMD>, no_side_effect, symbol = "aclshmem_consume_token_half_ptr_2d"} "dist.aclshmem_consume_token_half_ptr_2d" ins(%a_ptrs_76, %token_34 : tensor<128x32x!tt.ptr<f16>>, i32) outs(%a_ptrs_82 : tensor<128x32x!tt.ptr<f16>>) -> tensor<128x32x!tt.ptr<f16>>```2. this custom op will return !tt.ptr value, if it is used by tt.load, it will enter in BlockPtrAnalysis```%remote_mem_ptr = hivm.hir.custom {hivm.is_distributed, hivm.pipe = #hivm.pipe<PIPE_S>, hivm.tcore_type = #hivm.tcore_type<CUBE_AND_VECTOR>, hivm.vf_mode = #hivm.vf_mode<SIMD>, no_side_effect, symbol = "aclshmem_ptr_int64"} "dist.aclshmem_ptr_int64" ins(%remote_ptr, %target_rank : !tt.ptr<i64>, i32) -> !tt.ptr<i64>```in these two analysis, they will throw error, because they lack parseCustomOp

title: Supports pure Ascend (MSPTI only) without requiring CUDA/ROCm dev packages
body: Supports pure Ascend (MSPTI only) without requiring CUDA/ROCm dev packagesUses lightweight header + source stubs only when backends are disabledKeeps simple and readable CMake logicForces all vendor includesIt does FATAL_ERROR if CUPTI_INCLUDE_DIR or ROCTRACER_INCLUDE_DIR are not defined. This makes it impossible to build a clean Ascend-only version (PROTON_ENABLE_CUPTI=OFF, PROTON_ENABLE_ROCTRACER=OFF).Always pulls in ROCTRACER headersEvery object library gets:cmaketarget_include_directories(... SYSTEM PRIVATE "${ROCTRACER_INCLUDE_DIR}")Even when AMD backend is disabled.Overly complex structureUses global properties + add_proton_library() function + subdirectories + collecting object files. This adds unnecessary complexity compared to a simple file(GLOB) + direct add_library(proton SHARED ...) approach.PR https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/135

title: [Bug] Source line information lost for ops eliminated during compilation — many kernel lines unbreakable in msdebug
body: ### Describe the bug## SummaryMany Triton ops are eliminated, folded, absorbed, or otherwise consumed during the compilation pipeline. Each elimination silently drops the op's source `loc(...)`, so the corresponding kernel line never reaches the `.npubin`'s DWARF line table. The result: msdebug cannot set breakpoints on a significant fraction of source lines in a typical kernel, with no indication to the user that the line was elided rather than truly empty.```(msdebug) b kernel.py:6No locations (pending breakpoint).```The user has no way to tell, by looking at their kernel, whether a missing breakpoint means "this line really does nothing" or "the compiler ate the carrier for this line."## Affected categoriesWe hit this systematically across multiple op classes. Each category loses lines for a different reason:1. **Replaced ops** — `tt.get_program_id`, `tt.get_num_programs` and similar are replaced with direct kernel-argument reads. The original op is erased; its loc goes with it.2. **Folded ops in the Triton dialect** — `SplatOp::fold` and `BroadcastOp::fold` collapse splats/broadcasts of constants into `arith.constant dense<...>` *before* any Ascend pass runs. The fold's materialized constant carries the consumer's loc, not the folded op's.3. **Absorbed pointer arithmetic** — `tt.addptr`'s offset (e.g. `block_start + arange(0, N)`, or 2D `row[:,None]*BLOCK + col[None,:]`) is consumed analytically by `BlockPtrAnalysis` and rolled into `memref.reinterpret_cast` strides/offsets. The defining ops (`arith.muli`, `arith.addi`, `tt.make_range`, `tt.expand_dims`) are erased before any converter sees them.4. **Layout-only ops that survive but are optimized away later** — `tt.trans`, `tt.reshape`, `tt.expand_dims` lower to `linalg.transpose`/`tensor.reshape`/etc. in TTAdapter, then bishengir eliminates them downstream as zero-instruction transforms.5. **Callsite-wrapped library ops** — `tl.sum` and other helpers from `triton/language/standard.py` produce `tt.reduce` whose loc is `callsite(standard.py:N at kernel.py:M)`. DWARF emission resolves this to the library frame (standard.py), not the kernel frame, so the kernel line is missing while a useless library line appears instead.6. **Fused locations** — when two source-level ops collapse into one op (e.g. `tl.full` + `tl.broadcast_to` → single `linalg.fill`), the survivor carries a `FusedLoc[line_A, line_B]`. bishengir's DWARF emitter collapses `FusedLoc` to the function-level loc, losing both inner lines.## ReproducerA kernel exercising all the categories above:```pythonimport torch, tritonimport triton.language as tl@triton.jitdef k(xp, op4, op1, n, BLOCK: tl.constexpr, ROWS: tl.constexpr):    pid  = tl.program_id(0)                                 # category 1    np_  = tl.num_programs(0)                               # category 1    offs = pid * BLOCK + tl.arange(0, BLOCK)                # category 3 (1D)    one  = tl.full((BLOCK,), 1.0, dtype=tl.float32)         # category 2    x    = tl.load(xp + offs)    bcast = tl.broadcast_to(tl.reshape(x, (1, BLOCK)), (ROWS, BLOCK))   # category 4    trans = tl.trans(tl.reshape(x, (1, BLOCK)))             # category 4    row   = tl.arange(0, ROWS)    offs2 = row[:, None] * BLOCK + tl.arange(0, BLOCK)[None, :]   # category 3 (2D)    s     = tl.sum(bcast.to(tl.float32), axis=1)            # category 5    tl.store(op1 + offs,  x + one + tl.sum(trans.to(tl.float32), axis=1)[:, 0])    tl.store(op4 + offs2, bcast + s[:, None])``````bashmsdebug python3 kernel.py(msdebug) b kernel.py:6(msdebug) r```Compile, then:```bashreadelf --debug-dump=decodedline cache/<HASH>/k.npubin | grep <kernel.py>```Compare against the lines present in the TTIR `loc(...)` references. The difference is the lines that vanished — at the time of this report, ~8 distinct categories of loss were reproducible with kernels along these lines.## Why it matters- Source-level debugging is the primary way users diagnose kernel issues, especially perf and correctness bugs that only show up on real hardware (where `TRITON_INTERPRET` doesn't help).- The loss is silent: no warning, no indication. Users blame their setup or their syntax when in fact the compiler ate the line.- The pattern of loss is non-obvious: visually "trivial" lines (a `tl.trans`, a `tl.full`) seem like they should work, but don't, while "complex" lines (loads, atomics) tend to work fine. The mental model is exactly inverted from where users expect breakpoints to land.## Current workaround (PR #151)When `TRITON_DEBUG=1` is set, the Ascend pipeline emits `llvm.inline_asm "nop"` markers tagged with the original op's loc, before that op is replaced/absorbed/erased. The NOPs become real PCs in the `.npubin`, each carrying a DWARF entry for its source line. With `TRITON_DEBUG` unset, no NOPs are emitted and the binary is bit-identical to the pre-patch output.### Environment details- triton-ascend `release/3.5.x`- 910B4- LLVM/MLIR `fad32722`- TRITON_DISABLE_FFTS=0- TRITON_DEBUG=1- TRITON_DISABLE_LINE_INFO=0- TRITON_KERNEL_DUMP=1- TRITON_ALWAYS_COMPILE=1- TRITON_DEVICE_PRINT=1

title: FlashAttentionScore算子开箱性能很差
body: ### Describe the issue@triton.autotune(     configs=[           triton.Config({'BQ': 256, 'BK': 128}, num_warps=8),           triton.Config({'BQ': 128, 'BK': 64}, num_warps=8),           triton.Config({'BQ': 64, 'BK': 64}, num_warps=8),           triton.Config({'BQ': 64, 'BK': 32}, num_warps=4),           triton.Config({'BQ': 32, 'BK': 64}, num_warps=4),           triton.Config({'BQ': 32, 'BK': 32}, num_warps=4),           triton.Config({'BQ': 16, 'BK': 16}, num_warps=2),       ],       key=['n', 'D'],  # 当 n 或 D 变化时重新调优  )  @triton.jit  def flash_attention_forward_kernel(       q,       k,       v,       out,       l,       stride_qb,       stride_qn,       stride_qd,       stride_kb,       stride_kn,       stride_kd,       stride_vb,       stride_vn,       stride_vd,       stride_ob,       stride_on,       stride_od,       stride_lb,       stride_ln,       n: tl.int32,       d_scale: tl.float32,       IS_CAUSAL: tl.constexpr,       BQ: tl.constexpr,       BK: tl.constexpr,       D: tl.constexpr,       eps: tl.constexpr,   ):     pid_b = tl.program_id(0)    pid_tq = tl.program_id(1)    q_block_ptr = tl.make_block_ptr(         base=q + pid_b * stride_qb,         shape=(n, D),         strides=(stride_qn, stride_qd),         offsets=(pid_tq * BQ, 0),        block_shape=(BQ, D),         order=(1, 0),     )     k_block_ptr = tl.make_block_ptr(         base=k + pid_b * stride_kb,         shape=(D, n),         strides=(stride_kd, stride_kn),         offsets=(0, 0),        block_shape=(D, BK),         order=(0, 1),     )     v_block_ptr = tl.make_block_ptr(         base=v + pid_b * stride_vb,         shape=(n, D),        strides=(stride_vn, stride_vd),         offsets=(0, 0),        block_shape=(BK, D),         order=(1, 0),     )     o_block_ptr = tl.make_block_ptr(         base=out + pid_b * stride_ob,         shape=(n, D),         strides=(stride_on, stride_od),         offsets=(pid_tq * BQ, 0),         block_shape=(BQ, D),         order=(1, 0),     )     l_ptrs = l + pid_b * stride_lb + (pid_tq * BQ + tl.arange(0, BQ))     m_i = tl.full([BQ], value=float("-inf"), dtype=tl.float32)     l_i = tl.zeros([BQ], dtype=tl.float32)     o_i = tl.zeros([BQ, D], dtype=tl.float32)     q_i = tl.load(q_block_ptr, boundary_check=(0, 1))     q_i *= d_scale     j = 0     loop_end = tl.cdiv(n, BK)     if IS_CAUSAL:         loop_end = tl.cdiv((pid_tq + 1) * BQ, BK)     for j in range(loop_end):         k_j = tl.load(k_block_ptr, boundary_check=(0, 1))         v_j = tl.load(v_block_ptr, boundary_check=(0, 1))         s_ij = tl.dot(q_i, k_j)         if IS_CAUSAL:             offs_q = pid_tq * BQ + tl.arange(0, BQ)             offs_k = j * BK + tl.arange(0, BK)             causal_mask = offs_q[:, None] >= offs_k[None, :]             s_ij = tl.where(causal_mask, s_ij, -1e9)         m_new = tl.maximum(m_i, tl.max(s_ij, axis=1))         scale = tl.exp(m_i - m_new)         p_ij = tl.exp(s_ij - m_new[:, None])         l_new = scale * l_i + tl.sum(p_ij, axis=1)         o_i = scale[:, None] * o_i + tl.dot(p_ij.to(v_j.dtype), v_j)         l_i = l_new         m_i = m_new         k_block_ptr = tl.advance(k_block_ptr, (0, BK))         v_block_ptr = tl.advance(v_block_ptr, (BK, 0))     o_i /= l_i[:, None]     l_i = m_i + tl.log(l_i + eps)     tl.store(o_block_ptr, o_i.to(out.dtype.element_ty), boundary_check=(0, 1))     tl.store(l_ptrs, l_i, mask=(pid_tq * BQ + tl.arange(0, BQ)) < n) class FlashAttention(torch.autograd.Function):     @staticmethod     def forward(         ctx,         q: Float[Tensor, "b n d"],         k: Float[Tensor, "b n d"],         v: Float[Tensor, "b n d"],         is_causal=False,         eps=1e-6,     ):         device = q.device         b, n, d = q.shape         scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(d)         out = torch.empty_like(q)         l = torch.empty((b, n), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)         grid = (b, triton.cdiv(n, 16))         flash_attention_forward_kernel[grid](             q,             k,             v,             out,             l,             q.stride(0),             q.stride(1),             q.stride(2),             k.stride(0),             k.stride(1),            k.stride(2),            v.stride(0),             v.stride(1),             v.stride(2),             out.stride(0),             out.stride(1),             out.stride(2),             l.stride(0),             l.stride(1),             d_scale=scale,             IS_CAUSAL=is_causal,             D=d,             eps=eps,         )         ctx.save_for_backward(q, k, v, l, out)         ctx.is_causal = is_causal         return out### Environment details950上和910B4上性能都特别差，如batch, seq_len, head_dim = 2, 2048, 256时，性能和torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention差十倍以上950上用的是CANN9.0Triton ascend 3.2.1

title: V1架构图捕获崩溃：设置 TRITON_DISABLE_FFTS=1 导致 Triton 算子底层流获取失败
body: ### Describe the bug### 🐛 Bug Description (问题描述)在昇腾 Atlas A3 环境下，使用 vLLM V1 架构启动服务时，如果环境中设置了 `export TRITON_DISABLE_FFTS=1`，会在图捕获阶段（`Capturing CUDA graphs`）发生崩溃。崩溃点稳定发生在 `split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope` 这个 Triton 融合算子上。底层报错提示为 `_npu_getCurrentRawStream` 获取 stream 失败。**关键排查线索：** 如果将该环境变量修改为 `export TRITON_DISABLE_FFTS=0`，则图捕获顺利通过，服务正常拉起。希望官方能排查一下关闭 FFTS 优化为何会影响 Torchair 捕获阶段的流获取逻辑。###  Reproduction Steps (复现步骤)使用以下环境变量和命令即可稳定复现（注意未开启 eager 模式）：```bashexport VLLM_USE_V1=1export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1export TRITON_DISABLE_FFTS=1  # <--- 触发 Bug 的核心条件export ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1vllm serve /mnt_juntuan/models/Qwen3-30B-A3B-w8a8 \       --host 0.0.0.0 \       --served-model-name qwen3_30b \       --trust-remote-code \       --max-num-seqs 100 \       --max-model-len 20480 \       --max-num-batched-tokens 16384 \       --tensor-parallel-size 2 \       --port 9098 \       --profiler-config '{"profiler": "torch", "torch_profiler_dir": "./vllm_profile/eager_V2", "torch_profiler_with_stack": true}' \       --no-enable-prefix-caching \       --async-scheduling \       --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 \       --additional-config '{"enable_cpu_binding":true}'```###  Error Logs (报错日志)(Worker_TP0 pid=276)   File "/vllm-workspace/vllm-ascend/vllm_ascend/ops/triton/linearnorm/split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope.py", line 315, in split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope_impl(Worker_TP0 pid=276)     split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope_kernel[grid]((Worker_TP0 pid=276)   File "/usr/local/python3.11.14/lib/python3.11/site-packages/triton/runtime/jit.py", line 592, in run(Worker_TP0 pid=276)     stream = driver.active.get_current_stream(device)(Worker_TP0 pid=276)   File "/usr/local/python3.11.14/lib/python3.11/site-packages/triton/backends/ascend/driver.py", line 219, in get_current_stream(Worker_TP0 pid=276)     return _npu_getCurrentRawStream(device)(Worker_TP0 pid=276) RuntimeError: The Inner error is reported as above. The process exits for this inner error......(Worker_TP0 pid=276) [ERROR] 2026-05-12-13:41:57 (PID:276, Device:0, RankID:-1) ERR00100 PTA call acl api failed.### Environment details### 🌍 Environment (运行环境)* **硬件设备:** Atlas A3 HDK NPU (SOC: ascend910_9391)* **CANN 版本:** 8.5.1 (Toolkit)* **vllm-ascend 版本:** 0.19.1rc1* **Triton 相关库版本 (pip list):**  - `triton`: 3.5.0  - `triton_ascend`: 3.2.1* **模型:** Qwen3-30B-A3B-w8a8

title: [Feature] Support DescriptorScatterOp
body: # DescriptorScatterOp Lowering Scheme## Background`descriptor_scatter(desc, value, x_offsets, y_offset)` is a 2D scatter interface based on a tensor descriptor. It is used to write a 2D input block back to selected rows of a target tensor according to a given set of row indices, while writing from a shared starting offset in the column dimension.## Interface SemanticsIn TTIR, `descriptor_scatter(desc, value, x_offsets, y_offset)` is lowered to:```texttt.descriptor_scatter desc(x_offsets, y_offset) src```The four parameters mean:- `desc`: a 2D tensor descriptor- `src`: the 2D tensor to be written back- `x_offsets`: a 1D tensor of row indices indicating the target row set for write-back- `y_offset`: the starting offset in the column dimension, indicating where each row starts writingUsage constraints:- `desc` must correspond to a 2D tensor- `desc.block_shape[0] == 1`Its semantics are equivalent to:```textfor i in range(src.shape[0]):    descriptor_store(desc, src[i, :], [x_offsets[i], y_offset])```Where:- `src.shape[0] = len(x_offsets)`- `src.shape[1] = desc.block_shape[1]`Scatter means writing each row of `src` to a specified row of the target tensor, while using the same contiguous block in the column dimension.## Current Lowering Scheme### Overall IdeaA single `DescriptorScatterOp` is lowered into:- An `scf.for` loop that iterates over `src` row by row- In each iteration, one row block is extracted from `src`- A single-row write-back tensor pointer is constructed using `triton::MakeTensorPtrOp`- A single-row write-back is performed using `triton::StoreOp`In other words, scatter is lowered into a combination of multiple single-row write-backs inside a loop.### Detailed Steps#### 1. Unpack the descriptorFirst, extract the following from `MakeTensorDescOp`:- `base`- `shape`- `strides`- `padding`Corresponding helper function:- `unpackDescriptor(...)`#### 2. Extract the single-row block shapeFrom the descriptor type, obtain:- `rowBlockShape`It represents the block shape written by one scatter iteration, with value:```text[1, BLOCK_Y]```Where:- The first dimension is fixed to `1`- The second dimension is the contiguous write length in the column dimension, `BLOCK_Y`#### 3. Build the row-wise loopCreate an `scf.for` loop with:- lower bound = `0`- upper bound = `src.shape[0]`- step = `1`The loop variable `rowIv` indicates which row of `src` is currently being processed.#### 4. Extract the target row index for the current rowInside the loop body, extract the write-back target row index corresponding to the current row from `x_offsets`:```textxOffset = tensor.extract x_offsets[rowIv]````xOffset` means that `src[rowIv, :]` will eventually be written to row `xOffset` of the target tensor.#### 5. Construct a single-row tensor pointerUse `xOffset` and the shared `y_offset` to construct a `triton::MakeTensorPtrOp`:- row index: `xOffset`- column index: `y_offset`- tensor shape: `rowBlockShape`This constructs the pointer position of the current row write-back target tensor, that is, the pointer to a contiguous block on one row of the destination tensor.#### 6. Extract the current row block from the source tensorInside the loop body, extract the current row to be written back from `src`:```textrowValue = tensor.extract_slice(src, [rowIv, 0], [1, BLOCK_Y], [1, 1])```The resulting type is:```texttensor<1 x BLOCK_Y x elem_ty>```#### 7. Perform the single-row storeBased on `tensorPtr` and `rowValue` from the previous steps, perform one `triton::StoreOp`.This store follows the same behavior as the existing descriptor store, including:- `boundaryCheck`- `cacheModifier`- `evictionPolicy`The operation completed in one loop iteration is:```textstore rowValue -> desc[xOffset, y_offset : y_offset + BLOCK_Y]```#### 8. Erase the original op after the loop finishesSince scatter has in-place write-back semantics and does not produce a new tensor result, the original `DescriptorScatterOp` must be erased after the loop is created:```textrewriter.eraseOp(op)```## Equivalent PseudocodeThe whole lowering can be expressed as:```textdesc_info = unpack(desc)for row in range(src.shape[0]):    x = x_offsets[row]    ptr = make_tensor_ptr(        base = desc_info.base,        shape = desc_info.shape,        strides = desc_info.strides,        offsets = [x, y_offset],        tensor_shape = [1, BLOCK_Y]    )    row_value = src[row:row + 1, 0:BLOCK_Y]    store(ptr, row_value, boundary_check, cache_modifier, eviction_policy)```

title: [Feature request] triton-ascend need to support grid size >= 65536
body: SGLang project on NPU observed that triton-ascend does not support grid size >= 65536. This is required for long context LLMsSGLang issue https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/22023grid is (8192, 8)

title: [Docs] Refine CODEOWNERS for `third_party/ascend/`
body:
> **Status**: Open for discussion — scheduled for 2026-06-18 [Triton-Ascend BiWeekly Meeting](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/blob/main/docs/zh/community/community_technical_meeting.md).> **Goal**: Decide ownership and assign committers for `third_party/ascend/`.---## 1. Background & Problem### Current StateThe `.github/CODEOWNERS` file currently assigns a single owner to the entire`third_party/ascend/` directory:```gitignore# -----------# third_party# -----------third_party/amd/ @TecJeshthird_party/proton/ @TecJeshthird_party/ascend/ @HEX1A0A```### Problem1. **Owner no longer active on triton-ascend.** `@HEX1A0A` is no longer contributing to this repository day-to-day. 2. **Scope is too broad.** `third_party/ascend/` spans five distinct functional domains (see Section 2 below). A single owner cannot realistically be the domain expert for all of them.3. **Inconsistency with upstream patterns.** Currently, one backend in the upstream is assigned to multiple experts---## 2. Directory Breakdown### 2.1 Full Structure```bashthird_party/ascend/│├── .gitignore├── CMakeLists.txt # Top-level build integration├── ascend_ir.cc # Ascend IR bindings (C extension)├── triton_ascend.cc # Top-level Ascend backend entry point├── AscendNPU-IR/ # [Submodule] Upstream AscendNPU-IR├── backend/ # Python backend — compiler, driver, runtime, autotune│├── include/ # C++ headers — MLIR passes & dialects│ ├── AutoBlockify/│ ├── Dialect/│ ├── DiscreteMaskAccessConversion/│ ├── DynamicCVPipeline/│ ├── TritonAffinityOpt/│ ├── TritonToAnnotation/│ ├── TritonToGraph/│ ├── TritonToHFusion/│ ├── TritonToHIVM/│ ├── TritonToLLVM/│ ├── TritonToLinalg/│ ├── TritonToStructured/│ ├── TritonToUnstructure/│ └── Utils/│├── lib/ # C++ source — MLIR pass implementations│ ├── AutoBlockify/│ ├── Dialect/│ ├── DiscreteMaskAccessConversion/│ ├── DynamicCVPipeline/│ ├── TritonAffinityOpt/│ ├── TritonToAnnotation/│ ├── TritonToGraph/│ ├── TritonToHFusion/│ ├── TritonToHIVM/│ ├── TritonToLLVM/│ ├── TritonToLinalg/│ ├── TritonToStructured/│ ├── TritonToUnstructure/│ └── Utils/│├── language/ # Python frontend — Ascend-specific Triton ops├── llvm_patch/ # Patches for Triton's LLVM fork├── tutorials/ # Example kernels for Ascend NPU└── unittest/ # Ascend-specific test suite```### 2.2 Functional GroupingThe directories naturally group into **five functional domains**:| Domain | Directories | Description | Typical PR frequency || ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- || **A. MLIR Dialect & Passes** | `include/`, `lib/` | MLIR pass pairs forlowering | High — core compiler changes || **B. Python Backend** | `backend/` | Backend registration, compilation, autotuner | High — driver/runtime changes || **C. Frontend Language** | `language/` | Ascend-specific extensions op, device library | Medium — API additions || **D. Build & Integration** | `CMakeLists.txt`, `ascend_ir.cc`, `triton_ascend.cc`, `llvm_patch/`, `AscendNPU-IR/` | Build system, C extension glue, LLVM patches, submodule | Low — infrequent changes || **E. Tests & Tutorials** | `unittest/`, `tutorials/` | Regression tests, operator tests, tutorial examples | High — test additions/updates |---## 3. Discussion Points for the Meeting### 3.1 Should we refine ownership?| Option | Pros | Cons || --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ || **Option 1: Keep single owner**
`third_party/ascend/ @new_owner` | Simple; no maintenance of granular rules | Still a single point of failure; no domain specialization || **Option 2: Assign to functional groups**
Separate owners for A/B/C/D/E | Domain experts review what they know; distributes load | Must maintain mapping when directories change; need consensus on who owns what || **Option 3: Assign to 2–3 co-owners**
`third_party/ascend/ @owner1 @owner2 @owner3` | Simple; redundancy; any one can review | No domain specialization; all three get pinged on every PR |### 3.2 Who should be assigned?Based on the current [MAINTAINERS.md](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/blob/main/MAINTAINERS.md):| Name | GitHub ID | Join Date ||---|---|---|| Chen Cheng | ccdedreams | 2025-09-27 || Lijuan Hai | hailijuan | 2025-09-27 || Tao Wang | winter77-x | 2025-09-27 || Yingcong Liang | wcleungaj-70 | 2025-09-27 || Jingchang Shi | jingchangshi | 2025-09-27 || Hao Zhou | HaoVincentZhou | 2025-09-27 || Kaipeng Xing | kpxing | 2025-09-27 || Zhijie Zhao | zhaozhijie | 2025-09-27 || Kaixin Yang | kaixin1976 | 2025-09-27 || Ce Zhu | Cadenza4287 | 2025-12-23 || Xuan Peng | HinPeng | 2025-12-23 || Ziqi Hong | hongziqi | 2026-03-26 || Peiji Chen | zackc6 | 2026-05-07 || Ingu Kung | kig9981 | 2026-05-08 || Tianyao Wu | WuTYSFG | 2026-05-11 || Yihan Zhou | KanuaK | 2026-05-11 |## 4. Meeting Notes### SummaryThe team voted to adopt `Option 3` (Multiple co-owners for full directory) which matches upstream community lightweight management practice. We will add multiple active core developers as shared CODEOWNERS for third_party/ascend/ to eliminate single-point review risk.Attendees who reached the final decision:* Hong Ziqi* Zhu Guanda* Zhou Yihan* Cheng Maofan* Wu Tianyao* Zhou Hao* Hu Qiao### Final CODEOWNERS assignments```gitignore# (to be filled after the meeting)third_party/ascend/ @KanuaK @WuTYSFG @hongziqi ```### Follow-upSubmit a dedicated PR to update the .github/CODEOWNERS file with the above new ownership configuration.

title: [Feat] Reduction of Intrusive Modifications in triton-ascend
body:
> **Status**: Active —— Ongoing initiative to systematically eliminate custom divergences from upstream Triton and align code behavior with community standards.---## 1. Background: Why Reduce Intrusive Modifications?The `triton-ascend` fork was originally created by branching from the upstreamTriton project and adding Ascend support through direct modifications tocore compiler and runtime files. Over time, these modifications accumulatedas **intrusive changes** — edits made directly to upstream source files ratherthan through extension part in `third_party/ascend`.This approach creates significant long-term costs:| Problem | Impact || -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- || **Upstream merge conflicts** | Every community change to a modified file produces conflicts that must be manually resolved. The larger the diff, the more frequent and expensive the conflicts. || **Inconsistent operator behavior across backends** | Intrusive tweaks to default logic (e.g., the Ascend-exclusive `care_padding` parameter enabling automatic zero-filling in `tl.load`) cause identical Triton code to produce different runtime results on Ascend versus other hardware backends. |### The GoalMinimize the divergence between `triton-ascend` and upstream Triton by:1. **Restore**: Remove dead/redundant patches and restore upstream native semantics wherever feasible to fully align with community behavior.2. **Isolate**: Migrate required Ascend-specific logic into the `third_party/ascend/` directory using monkey-patching, avoiding direct edits to upstream source files.3. **Retain only unavoidable patches**: Keep intrusive edits that are mandatory for Ascend hardware support and cannot be implemented via extension mechanisms, with full documented justification for retention.---## 2. Guiding Principles for Intrusive Modification Reduction### Principle 1: Remove Redundant Patches & Restore Upstream SemanticsTwo sub-scenarios fall under this unified alignment principle:1. Any intrusive code patch with zero runtime callers or duplicate functionality matching upstream shall be deleted with no migration work required. - Example: Redundant local `tl_cdiv` alias wrapper inside the Gluon module2. If a patch overrides upstream default logic that users can replicate via standard public APIs, revert to the community default and require users to explicitly opt into custom behavior through native parameters. - Examples: - Eliminate auto zero-padding in `_load_block_pointer`; users enable padding via `padding_option="zero"`### Principle 2: Isolate Ascend Logic via Monkey-PatchingWhen Ascend genuinely needs different behavior, but that behavior can beinjected at runtime through Python's dynamic dispatch, implement the changein `third_party/ascend/` and apply it via monkey-patch. This keeps upstreamsource files pristine.**Example pattern:**```python# third_party/ascend/backend/__init__.py# Monkey-patch inject Ascend device attribute logic without editing# upstream driver code.```### Principle 3: Preserve with JustificationIf an intrusive edit cannot be deleted, aligned with upstream, or isolated via monkey-patching, it may be retained subject to strict governance rules:- A clear, documented reason why it must remain- A plan for eventual elimination (e.g., wait for upstream feature)### Principle 4: Mandatory Pre-Merge Review for All New Intrusive ChangesWe strictly limit introducing new intrusive modifications to upstream core files. All newly added Ascend capabilities must be implemented within third_party/ascend/ or official upstream extension interfaces (backend plugin abstractions, pass registration hooks, etc.). Any proposed new intrusive patch requires review and approval before merge.---## 3. Unified 3-Category Classification & Resolution WorkflowEach intrusive change falls into one of four categories:```mermaidflowchart TD %% 样式美化 classDef decision fill:#e6f7ff,stroke:#1890ff classDef catA fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745 classDef catB fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107 classDef catC fill:#f8d7da,stroke:#dc3545 root{Can we align with upstream?}:::decision catA[Category A: Align & Purge
Remove dead code / Restore upstream defaults]:::catA judge{Isolatable via monkey-patch?}:::decision catB[Category B: Isolatable
Migrate logic to third_party/ascend]:::catB catC[Category C: Unavoidable Patch
Retain with full retention docs]:::catC root -- Yes --> catA root -- No --> judge judge -- Yes --> catB judge -- No --> catC```### Category A — Align & Purge (Remove or Restore Upstream Defaults)Covers two original merged cases:1. Dead unused code: Patches never invoked at runtime, no business dependencies; delete directly.2. Redundant divergent defaults: Patches overriding community behavior that users can replicate via standard APIs; revert to upstream native semantics.### Category B — Isolatable via Monkey-Patch (Migrate to third_party/)Ascend-specific logic that can be injected at runtime through Pythonmonkey-patching, hook registration, or backend plugin interfaces.### Category C — Unavoidable Patches (Retain with Full Documentation)Changes that are strictly required and cannot be removed, aligned, orisolated. Documented and reviewed periodically.---## 4. Completed & In-Progress Remediation PR List| PR | File | Status | author | category | Change | Rationale || ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ----------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- || [#617](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/617) | `python/triton/language/semantic.py` | checked | zhudada0120 | B(Isolatable) | Redundant `tl.int1` logic | Already covered by HF32 PR; removing with no test impact || [#517](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/517) | `python/src/ir.cc` | checked | TecJesh | A(Align & Purge) | fp8 type creation helpers | No business caller; dead code || [#582](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/582) | `python/triton/compiler/code_generator.py` | update pr description | TecJesh | A(Align & Purge) | Remove redundant `repr` in error messages | Upstream 3.5 provides `TRITON_FRONT_END_DEBUGGING` for full stack traces; local `repr` wrapping is unnecessary || [#584](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/584) | `python/triton/language/core.py` | fix confilct | TecJesh | A(Align & Purge) | Remove `fast_math` param | Ascend-local extension not in upstream; default `True` to maintain compatibility with existing tests || [#533](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/533) | `python/triton/language/semantic.py` | merged | hongziqi | A(Align & Purge) | Remove auto-disable of zero-padding for boundary OOB | Ascend had disabled upstream's default zero-fill on out-of-bounds load to avoid NaN in Qwen3. Users now control padding explicitly via `padding_option`. || [#515](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/515) | `bin/triton-opt.cpp` | merged | TecJesh | A(Align & Purge) | Register debug pass | Temporary debugging leftover; no runtime use || [#509](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/509) | `python/triton/experimental/gluon/language_standard.py` | merged | hongziqi | A(Align & Purge) | Redundant `tl_cdiv` alias | Local alias for same upstream function; Gluon restored to `_import_from_triton(tl_standard.cdiv)` || [#514](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/514) | `python/triton/language/core.py` | merged | hongziqi | A(Align & Purge) | `sanitize_overflow` default `False` → `True` | Upstream default is `True`. Ascend's `triton-to-linalg` pass erases overflow AssertOps; no functional impact in either production or debug mode. Two-layer protection: (1) `device_assert` is gated by `TRITON_DEBUG`; (2) even when debug is on, the Ascend backend filters overflow-detection asserts. || [#529](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/529) | `python/triton/language/core.py` | merged | hongziqi | A(Align & Purge) | Remove `care_padding` param from `tl.load` | Auto-zero-fill on masked load was an Ascend-only default. Users can pass `other=0` explicitly for identical behavior. Aligns `tl.load` signature and semantics with upstream. || [#475](https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/pull/475) | `python/triton/language/semantic.py` | merged | gymgit1
| A(Align & Purge) | Revert `atomic_cas` type check changes | Local modification was incorrect; restored upstream validation logic |

title: 910B上跑awq_dequantize_kernel算子报错，GPU上没有问题: error: 'memref.collapse_shape' op invalid source layout map or collapsing non-contiguous dims
body: ### Describe the bug### awq.py```pythonimport torchimport tritonimport triton.language as tlimport triton.runtime.driver as driverAWQ_TRITON_SUPPORTED_GROUP_SIZES = [32]@triton.jitdef awq_dequantize_kernel(    qweight_ptr,  # quantized matrix    scales_ptr,  # scales, per group    zeros_ptr,  # zeros, per group    group_size,  # Should always be one of the supported group sizes    result_ptr,  # Output matrix    num_cols,  # input num cols in qweight    num_rows,  # input num rows in qweight    total_tiles,       # 新增：总 tile 数，方便在 kernel 里直接用    BLOCK_SIZE_X: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_SIZE_Y: tl.constexpr,    NUM_CORES: tl.constexpr,   # num_core):    # Set up the pids.    pid = tl.program_id(axis=0)        # 提前算好 grid 维度，注意 triton.cdiv 或手动计算    # 这里假设 NUM_TILES_X, NUM_TILES_Y 作为 constexpr 或通过参数传入    # 为简化，用 tl.cdiv 在 kernel 内计算（需 triton 版本支持）    grid_x = tl.cdiv(num_cols, BLOCK_SIZE_X)    grid_y = tl.cdiv(num_rows, BLOCK_SIZE_Y)        # Create reverse AWQ order as tensor: [0, 4, 1, 5, 2, 6, 3, 7]    # that will map given indices to the correct order.    reverse_awq_order_tensor = (        (tl.arange(0, 2) * 4)[None, :] + tl.arange(0, 4)[:, None]    ).reshape(8)    # Use this to compute a set of shifts that can be used to unpack and    # reorder the values in iweights and zeros.    shifts = reverse_awq_order_tensor * 4    shifts = tl.broadcast_to(shifts[None, :], (BLOCK_SIZE_Y * BLOCK_SIZE_X, 8))    shifts = tl.reshape(shifts, (BLOCK_SIZE_Y, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8))        for tile_id in range(pid, total_tiles, NUM_CORES):        pid_x = tile_id % grid_x        pid_y = tile_id // grid_x        # Compute offsets and masks for qweight_ptr.        offsets_y = pid_y * BLOCK_SIZE_Y + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_Y)        offsets_x = pid_x * BLOCK_SIZE_X + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_X)        offsets = num_cols * offsets_y[:, None] + offsets_x[None, :]        masks_y = offsets_y < num_rows        masks_x = offsets_x < num_cols        masks = masks_y[:, None] & masks_x[None, :]        # Compute offsets and masks for result output ptr.        result_offsets_y = pid_y * BLOCK_SIZE_Y + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_Y)        result_offsets_x = pid_x * BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8 + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8)        result_offsets = (            8 * num_cols * result_offsets_y[:, None] + result_offsets_x[None, :]        )        result_masks_y = result_offsets_y < num_rows        result_masks_x = result_offsets_x < num_cols * 8        result_masks = result_masks_y[:, None] & result_masks_x[None, :]        # Load the weights.        iweights = tl.load(qweight_ptr + offsets, masks, 0.0)        iweights = tl.expand_dims(iweights, 2)                 # (Y, X, 1)        iweights = tl.broadcast_to(iweights, (BLOCK_SIZE_Y, BLOCK_SIZE_X, 8))  # (Y, X, 8)        iweights = tl.reshape(iweights, (BLOCK_SIZE_Y, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8))       # (Y, X*8)        # Unpack and reorder: shift out the correct 4-bit value and mask.        iweights = (iweights >> shifts) & 0xF        # Compute zero offsets and masks.        zero_offsets_y = pid_y * BLOCK_SIZE_Y // group_size + tl.arange(0, 1)        zero_offsets_x = pid_x * BLOCK_SIZE_X + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_X)        zero_offsets = num_cols * zero_offsets_y[:, None] + zero_offsets_x[None, :]        zero_masks_y = zero_offsets_y < num_rows // group_size        zero_masks_x = zero_offsets_x < num_cols        zero_masks = zero_masks_y[:, None] & zero_masks_x[None, :]        # Load the zeros.        zeros = tl.load(zeros_ptr + zero_offsets, zero_masks, 0.0)        zeros = tl.expand_dims(zeros, 2)                     # (1, X, 1)        zeros = tl.broadcast_to(zeros, (1, BLOCK_SIZE_X, 8)) # (1, X, 8)        zeros = tl.reshape(zeros, (1, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8))     # (1, X*8)        zeros = tl.broadcast_to(zeros, (BLOCK_SIZE_Y, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8))        # Unpack and reorder: shift out the correct 4-bit value and mask.        zeros = (zeros >> shifts) & 0xF        # Compute scale offsets and masks.        scale_offsets_y = pid_y * BLOCK_SIZE_Y // group_size + tl.arange(0, 1)        scale_offsets_x = pid_x * BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8 + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8)        scale_offsets = num_cols * 8 * scale_offsets_y[:, None] + scale_offsets_x[None, :]        scale_masks_y = scale_offsets_y < num_rows // group_size        scale_masks_x = scale_offsets_x < num_cols * 8        scale_masks = scale_masks_y[:, None] & scale_masks_x[None, :]        # Load the scales.        scales = tl.load(scales_ptr + scale_offsets, scale_masks, 0.0)        scales = tl.broadcast_to(scales, (BLOCK_SIZE_Y, BLOCK_SIZE_X * 8))        # Dequantize.        iweights = (iweights - zeros) * scales        iweights = iweights.to(result_ptr.type.element_ty)        # Finally, store.        tl.store(result_ptr + result_offsets, iweights, result_masks)# qweights - [K     , M // 8], int32# scales   - [K // G, M     ], float16# zeros    - [K // G, M // 8], int32def awq_dequantize_triton(    qweight: torch.Tensor,    scales: torch.Tensor,    zeros: torch.Tensor,    block_size_x: int = 16,    block_size_y: int = 16,) -> torch.Tensor:    K = qweight.shape[0]    M = scales.shape[1]    group_size = qweight.shape[0] // scales.shape[0]    assert K > 0 and M > 0    assert scales.shape[0] == K // group_size and scales.shape[1] == M    assert zeros.shape[0] == K // group_size and zeros.shape[1] == M // 8    assert group_size <= K    assert group_size in AWQ_TRITON_SUPPORTED_GROUP_SIZES or group_size == K    # Result tensor:    # number of rows = same as input tensor    # number of cols = 8 x input tensor num cols    result = torch.empty(        qweight.shape[0],        qweight.shape[1] * 8,        device=qweight.device,        dtype=scales.dtype,    )    Y = qweight.shape[0]  # num rows    X = qweight.shape[1]  # num cols        # Launch kernel    num_core = get_npu_properties()["num_vectorcore"] # 获取vectorcore数量    grid = (num_core, )    grid_x = triton.cdiv(X, block_size_x)    grid_y = triton.cdiv(Y, block_size_y)    total_tiles = grid_x * grid_y        awq_dequantize_kernel[grid](        qweight,        scales,        zeros,        group_size,        result,        X,        Y,        total_tiles,        BLOCK_SIZE_X=block_size_x,        BLOCK_SIZE_Y=block_size_y,        NUM_CORES=num_core,    )    return result```### test.py```pythondef test_dequantize(qweight_rows, qweight_cols, group_size):    if group_size == -1:        group_size = qweight_rows    qweight_dtype = torch.int32    scales_rows = qweight_rows // group_size    scales_cols = qweight_cols * 8    scales_dtype = torch.float16    zeros_rows = scales_rows    zeros_cols = qweight_cols    zeros_dtype = torch.int32    set_random_seed(0)    qweight = torch.randint(        0,        torch.iinfo(torch.int32).max,        (qweight_rows, qweight_cols),        dtype=qweight_dtype,        device=device,    )    scales = torch.rand(scales_rows, scales_cols, dtype=scales_dtype, device=device)    zeros = torch.randint(        0,        torch.iinfo(torch.int32).max,        (zeros_rows, zeros_cols),        dtype=zeros_dtype,        device=device,    )    iweights_triton = awq_dequantize_triton(qweight, scales, zeros)    assert not torch.any(torch.isinf(iweights_triton)) and not torch.any(        torch.isnan(iweights_triton)    )    iweights_torch = awq_dequantize_torch(qweight, scales, zeros, group_size)    torch.testing.assert_close(iweights_triton, iweights_torch)test_dequantize(128, 128, 32)```报错的位置应该是```pythoniweights = body: ## BackgroundTriton-Ascend currently handles most Triton memory accesses through the SIMD-oriented lowering path. For kernels that contain indirect or semi-structured memory access patterns, this path either falls back to scalarized loop expansion or requires the SIMT template path, which limits the ability to use native HFusion gather/scatter operations.We need a compile mode that can keep the normal SIMD pipeline for structured computation while routing unstructured load/store operations to a SIMT-capable or HFusion-native path.## GoalAdd a `compile_mode="simd_simt"` option that enables mixed SIMD/SIMT compilation for unstructured memory access on 910_95-class targets. In this mode, unstructured `tl.load` / `tl.store` patterns should be recognized, represented explicitly in the IR, and lowered to `hfusion.gather_load` / `hfusion.scatter_store`, using a conservative `burstlen=1` when the burst length cannot be statically determined.## Scope- Introduce compile-mode based routing for `simd`, `simd_simt`, `simt_template`, and existing compatibility modes.- Identify unstructured pointer offsets and discrete-mask memory accesses before Linalg lowering.- Add intermediate `ascend.unstructured_load` / `ascend.unstructured_store` ops to carry base pointer, indices, mask, and unstructured-dim metadata.- Lower intermediate ops to HFusion gather/scatter in `simd_simt` mode, with fallback to SIMT template calls when needed.- Keep `force-simt-template` accepted as a deprecated pass-option alias for existing MLIR pipelines.- Add tests covering indirect load/store, index select/put style semi-structured access, pass option compatibility, and MLIR conversion behavior.## Acceptance Criteria- `compile_mode="simd_simt"` is rejected on unsupported A2/A3 targets and accepted on 910_95-class targets.- Unstructured load/store ops are produced by `TritonToUnstructure` in mix compile modes.- `TritonToLinalg` lowers static-burst unstructured ops to HFusion gather/scatter in `simd_simt` mode.- Dynamic burst-length cases do not emit invalid constants; they conservatively use `burstlen=1` on the HFusion gather/scatter path.- Existing `simd` behavior remains unchanged.- Existing `force-simt-template` MLIR pass pipelines remain parseable during the transition to `compile-mode`.

title: triton-opt and bishengir-compile command lines printed with TRITON_DEBUG=1 should be copy-pastable without changes
body: If `TRITON_DEBUG=1` is set, we print `triton-opt` and `bishengir-compile` command lines that greatly aid debugging:```TRITON_ALWAYS_COMPILE=1 TRITON_DEBUG=1 TRITON_DUMP_DIR=./dump python3 01-vector-add.py...[DEBUG] cmd list: /home/abokhanko/bisheng/triton-ascend/build/lib.linux-x86_64-cpython-311/triton/_C/triton-opt /tmp/tmpeeekbnge/kernel.ttir.mlir '--pass-pipeline=any(auto-blockify{auto-blockify-size=1},triton-to-structured{enable-mask-fallback-conversion=false optimize-dynamic-offset=false},discrete-mask-access-conversion{compile-on-910-95=false enable-sync-block-lock=false force-simt-template=false},triton-to-annotation,triton-to-unstructure{compile-on-910-95=false force-scalarize-mode=false force-simt-template=false},triton-to-hivm,triton-to-hfusion,triton-to-llvm,bubble-up-operation{enable-aggressive-mode=true},triton-to-structured{enable-mask-fallback-conversion=false optimize-dynamic-offset=false},triton-to-linalg{compile-on-910-95=false enable-nd2nz-on-vector=false enable-select-analysis=true global-kernel=false named-ops=true})' --mlir-print-debuginfo -o /tmp/tmpeeekbnge/kernel.ttadapter.mlir[DEBUG] cmd_list: /home/abokhanko/bisheng/AscendNPU-IR/build/bin/bishengir-compile /tmp/tmpzz5sbupb/kernel.mlir --target=Ascend910B4-1 --enable-auto-multi-buffer=True --enable-auto-bind-sub-block=True --link-aicore-bitcode=/home/abokhanko/bisheng/triton-ascend/build/lib.linux-x86_64-cpython-311/triton/backends/ascend/lib/libdevice.10.bc --enable-hfusion-compile=true --enable-hivm-compile=true --enable-triton-kernel-compile=true --bishengir-print-ir-after=hivm-graph-sync-solver -o /tmp/tmpzz5sbupb/kernel```These lines are not copy-pastable without changes, though, for [at least] two reasons:1) They refer to files in `/tmp/something/` dirs, even when `TRITON_DUMP_DIR` is set.2) Options might include spaces inside (for example, `--append-bisheng-options=-cce-link-aicore-ll-module file.bc` added at https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/blob/016bec131df8ec738a4e4f705c6a939c4a6e23bc/third_party/ascend/backend/compiler.py#L814).This should be fixed and the printed command lines should be copy-pastable without any changes at all.
