title: TRITON_DEBUG and @triton.jit(debug=True) can produce conflicting debug states
body: ### Describe the bug## DescriptionThere is currently a mismatch between the value of the `TRITON_DEBUG` environment variable and the effective compiler debug option.For example:```pythonimport triton@triton.jit(debug=True)def kernel(...):    ...```and```bashexport TRITON_DEBUG=0```result in the following state inside `compiler.py`:```textTRITON_DEBUG=Falseopt.debug=True```This creates an inconsistent debug configuration because some parts of the compilation pipeline may rely on `opt.debug`, while others may directly read `TRITON_DEBUG`.## Expected behaviorThere should be a single source of truth for the debug mode.Possible approaches:1. Treat `opt.debug` as the authoritative value and avoid reading `TRITON_DEBUG` directly in the backend and C++ passes.2. Synchronize `TRITON_DEBUG` and `opt.debug` during compilation.3. At minimum, emit a warning when the two values differ.## Why this mattersThe current behavior can lead to situations where:* kernel compilation runs in debug mode (`opt.debug=True`),* backend passes or utilities behave as if debug mode is disabled (`TRITON_DEBUG=False`),This makes debugging behavior difficult to reason about and may produce inconsistent dumps, logging, or optimization decisions.### Environment detailstriton-ascend: commit 04f6e81c6b04c77a9aa9f953e088fde2398f89d2

title: 950PR上跑fp8算子coredump，报错Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed
body: ### Describe the bug算子代码如下：# Copyright © 2026 Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.import mathimport torchimport tritonimport triton.language as tlDEVICE = (    "cuda"    if torch.cuda.is_available()    else "npu"    if torch.npu.is_available()    else "cpu")@triton.jitdef _per_token_group_quant_fp8(    x_ptr,    x_q_ptr,    x_s_ptr,    group_size,    num_groups,    y_num_columns,    y_row_stride,    eps,    fp8_min: tl.constexpr,    fp8_max: tl.constexpr,    use_ue8m0: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,    N: tl.constexpr,):    groups_per_row = y_num_columns // group_size    local_pid = tl.program_id(0)    M = tl.num_programs(0)    # ====================== 循环处理多个 group ======================    for iN in range(N):        # 正确计算全局 group ID        g_id = local_pid + iN * M        # 计算属于第几行、第几个group        row = g_id // groups_per_row        row_g_id = g_id % groups_per_row        # ====================== 【关键】每次循环都重新计算指针，不使用 += ======================        x_g_ptr = x_ptr + (row * y_row_stride) + (row_g_id * group_size)        x_q_g_ptr = x_q_ptr + g_id * group_size        x_s_g_ptr = x_s_ptr + g_id        # 加载        cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)        mask = (cols < group_size) & (g_id < num_groups)        x = tl.load(x_g_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0)        # 量化计算        _absmax = tl.maximum(tl.max(tl.abs(x)), eps)        scale_raw = _absmax * (1.0 / fp8_max)        x_s = tl.math.exp2(tl.ceil(tl.log2(scale_raw))) if use_ue8m0 else scale_raw        x_q = tl.clamp(x / x_s, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(x_q_ptr.dtype.element_ty)        # 写回（每次都用新指针，不叠加）        tl.store(x_q_g_ptr + cols, x_q, mask=mask)        tl.store(x_s_g_ptr, x_s, mask=g_id < num_groups)@triton.jitdef _per_token_group_quant_fp8_colmajor(    x_ptr,    x_q_ptr,    x_s_ptr,    group_size,    num_groups,    y_num_columns,    y_row_stride,    y_s_col_stride,    eps,    fp8_min: tl.constexpr,    fp8_max: tl.constexpr,    use_ue8m0: tl.constexpr,    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,    N: tl.constexpr,):    groups_per_row = y_num_columns // group_size    local_pid = tl.program_id(0)    M = tl.num_programs(0)    for iN in range(N):        g_id = local_pid + iN * M        row = g_id // groups_per_row        row_g_id = g_id % groups_per_row        # ====================== 【关键】每次循环重新计算，不 += ======================        x_g_ptr = x_ptr + row * y_row_stride + row_g_id * group_size        x_q_g_ptr = x_q_ptr + g_id * group_size        blocks_per_row = groups_per_row        scale_col = g_id % blocks_per_row        scale_row = g_id // blocks_per_row        # scale 指针正确计算        scale_offset = scale_col * y_s_col_stride + scale_row        x_s_g_ptr = x_s_ptr + scale_offset        cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)        mask = (cols < group_size) & (g_id < num_groups)        x = tl.load(x_g_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0)        _absmax = tl.maximum(tl.max(tl.abs(x)), eps)        scale_raw = _absmax * (1.0 / fp8_max)        x_s = tl.math.exp2(tl.ceil(tl.log2(scale_raw))) if use_ue8m0 else scale_raw        x_q = tl.clamp(x / x_s, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(x_q_ptr.dtype.element_ty)        tl.store(x_q_g_ptr + cols, x_q, mask=mask)        tl.store(x_s_g_ptr, x_s, mask=g_id < num_groups)def triton_per_token_group_quant_fp8(    x: torch.Tensor,    group_size: int,    dtype: torch.dtype,    column_major_scales: bool = False,    block_size=None,    eps: float = 1e-10,    use_ue8m0: bool = False,) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:    assert x.shape[-1] % group_size == 0, (        f"the last dimension of `x` {x.shape[-1]} must be divisible "        f"by `group_size` {group_size}"    )    assert x.stride(-1) == 1, "`x` must be contiguous on the last dimension"    x = x.to(DEVICE)    fp8_min = torch.finfo(dtype).min    fp8_max = torch.finfo(dtype).max    # 输出tensor分配    x_q = torch.empty(x.shape, device=x.device, dtype=dtype)    if column_major_scales:        shape = x.shape[:-2] + (x.shape[-1] // group_size, x.shape[-2])        x_s = torch.empty(shape, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype).permute(-1, -2)    else:        shape = x.shape[:-1] + (x.shape[-1] // group_size,)        x_s = torch.empty(shape, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)    num_groups = x.numel() // group_size    # Grid 配置（NPU自动获取VectorCore数量）    if DEVICE == "cuda":        block_size = triton.next_power_of_2(group_size)        N = 1        grid = (num_groups,)    else:        block_size = group_size        import triton.runtime.driver as driver        device_idx = torch.npu.current_device()        core_num = driver.active.utils.get_device_properties(device_idx)[            "num_vectorcore"        ]        grid = (core_num,)        N = (num_groups + core_num - 1) // core_num    # 启动kernel    if column_major_scales:        _per_token_group_quant_fp8_colmajor[grid](            x,            x_q,            x_s,            group_size,            num_groups,            x.shape[1],            x.stride(0),            x_s.stride(1),            eps,            fp8_min,            fp8_max,            use_ue8m0,            block_size,            N,        )    else:        # 【修复】统一使用grid启动，不写死(M,)        _per_token_group_quant_fp8[grid](            x,            x_q,            x_s,            group_size,            num_groups,            x.shape[1],            x.stride(0),            eps,            fp8_min,            fp8_max,            use_ue8m0,            block_size,            N,        )    return x_q, x_s测试代码如下：NUM_TOKENS = [7, 2050]DIMS = [512, 4096, 5120, 13824]GROUP_SIZES = [64, 128, 512]# DTYPES = [torch.float16, torch.float8_e5m2, torch.float8_e4m3fn]DTYPES = [torch.float8_e5m2, torch.float8_e4m3fn]COLUMN_MAJOR_SCALES = [True, False]SEEDS = [123]# 精度标准RTOL = {torch.float32: 1e-3, torch.float16: 1e-1}ATOL = {torch.float32: 1e-4, torch.float16: 1e-1}# 性能测试标准PERFORMANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.9  # 0.9 * AscendCWARMUP = 0ITER = 1def benchmark_perf(num_tokens, ndim, group_size, block_size, dtype, provider):    print("====DTYPE={}".format(dtype))    shape = (num_tokens, ndim)    x = torch.rand(shape, dtype=torch.float32).to(dtype).to(DEVICE)    USE_AUTOTUNE = block_size == 0    if provider == "torch":        ms = triton.testing.do_bench(            lambda: torch_per_token_group_quant_fp8(x, group_size, dtype)        )    if provider == "triton":        if USE_AUTOTUNE:            ms = triton.testing.do_bench(                lambda: triton_per_token_group_quant_fp8(x, group_size, dtype)            )        else:            ms = triton.testing.do_bench(                lambda: triton_per_token_group_quant_fp8(                    x, group_size, dtype, block_size=block_size                )            )    return msconfigs = []for _num, _dim in [[2050, 13824], [500, 5120], [7, 512]]:    for _group_size in GROUP_SIZES:        for _dtype in DTYPES:            configs.append(                triton.testing.Benchmark(                    # argument names to use as an x-axis for the plot                    x_names=[                        "block_size",                    ],                    x_vals=[                        32,                        64,                        96,                        128,                        256,                        512,                    ],  # different possible values for `x_name`                    line_arg="provider",                    # argument name whose value corresponds to a different line in the plot                    # possible values for `line_arg``                    line_vals=["torch", "triton"],                    # label name for the lines                    line_names=["torch", "triton"],                    # line styles                    styles=[("green", "-"), ("blue", "-")],                    ylabel="runtime(ms)",  # label name for the y-axis                    plot_name=f"per_token_group_quant_fp8-{_num}-{_dim}-{_group_size}-{_dtype}",                    # name for the plot. Used also as a file name for saving the plot.                    args={                        "num_tokens": _num,                        "ndim": _dim,                        "dtype": _dtype,                        "group_size": _group_size,                    },                )            )@triton.testing.perf_report(configs)def do_bench_perf(num_tokens, ndim, group_size, block_size, dtype, provider):    return benchmark_perf(num_tokens, ndim, group_size, block_size, dtype, provider)if __name__ == "__main__":    # 不输入参数, do_bench跑指定切块的性能    if len(sys.argv) == 1:        do_bench_perf.run(show_plots=True, print_data=True, save_path=".")### Environment detailstriton3.2.1950PR

title: [packaging] triton-ascend conflicts with mainline triton over triton/backends/compiler.py namespace
body: ## Summary`triton-ascend` 3.2.0/3.2.1 and mainline `triton` (3.3+) cannot coexist in the same site-packages. Both pip packages claim ownership of `triton/backends/compiler.py` and `triton/backends/__init__.py`, and the two forks of `compiler.py` have diverged enough that mainline Triton's `triton/backends/amd/compiler.py` fails to import once `triton-ascend` overwrites the file.Symptom — `import triton` raises:```ImportError: cannot import name 'Language' from 'triton.backends.compiler'  (/usr/local/python3.11.14/lib/python3.11/site-packages/triton/backends/compiler.py)  at triton.backends.__init__:_discover_backends  via triton.backends.amd.compiler line 1:    from triton.backends.compiler import BaseBackend, GPUTarget, Language```This is not specific to the AMD backend — `_discover_backends()` iterates all backends, so any `import triton` (Ascend or otherwise) fails.## Why this mattersDownstream NPU integrators frequently need both:- `triton-ascend` for the Triton DSL on Ascend- `vllm` / `vllm-ascend`, which transitively requires `xgrammar`, which in turn `Requires: triton` (mainline)The current packaging makes these requirements mutually incompatible by construction, even though the actual code surfaces don't conflict — only the namespace squat does. The user has to apply a fragile post-install fix (`pip uninstall triton && pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps triton-ascend`) after every environment recreation, which makes container builds and CI scripts brittle.I notice the project README announces a planned Triton 3.5 upgrade for 2026 — that's great, but the packaging conflict is orthogonal to which mainline Triton version you track. Even at Triton 3.5, the two `compiler.py` files will continue to diverge (Huawei-specific symbols like `AttrsDescriptor`, mainline-specific symbols like `Language`/[future]), so the underlying issue persists.## Environment- Ascend A3 NPU (dav-c220), CANN 8.5.0- Container: `quay.io/ascend/verl:verl-8.5.2-a3-ubuntu22.04-py3.11-qwen3-5`- Python 3.11.14- `pip show` reports:  - `triton 3.6.0` (Required-by: xgrammar; xgrammar Required-by: vllm, vllm_ascend)  - `triton-ascend 3.2.0`## Reproduce```bash# In a fresh Python env on NPU hostpip install vllm-ascend            # transitively installs xgrammar → triton 3.6.0pip install triton-ascend           # bundled compiler.py overwrites mainlinepython -c "import triton; print(triton.__version__)"# -> ImportError: cannot import name 'Language' from 'triton.backends.compiler'```## Root cause (verified)`pip show -f triton-ascend` reports:```triton/backends/__init__.pytriton/backends/compiler.pytriton/backends/ascend/__init__.pytriton/backends/ascend/compiler.py````pip show -f triton` reports:```triton/backends/__init__.pytriton/backends/compiler.pytriton/backends/amd/__init__.pytriton/backends/amd/compiler.pytriton/backends/nvidia/__init__.pytriton/backends/nvidia/compiler.py```The two `compiler.py` files have completely different exported symbols:| File source | Exports `class Language` | Exports `AttrsDescriptor` | Size (bytes) ||---|---|---|---|| Mainline `triton 3.6.0` (verified via `zipfile` read of the PyPI wheel) | ✅ | ❌ | 2,834 || `triton-ascend 3.2.0` (this package's bundled `python/triton/backends/compiler.py`) | ❌ | ✅ | 11,388 |Whichever package is installed later wins the file. If `triton-ascend` is the later install, mainline triton's `amd/compiler.py` and `nvidia/compiler.py` (still on disk, still discovered by `_discover_backends`) try to import `Language` from a file that no longer exports it → ImportError.`triton-ascend`'s pypi metadata (`pip show triton-ascend`) currently reports:```Provides-Dist: <none>Provides: <none>Conflicts: <none>```So pip's resolver treats `triton` and `triton-ascend` as independent packages and happily installs both. There's no signal to pip — or to downstream packages like xgrammar — that triton-ascend is a substitute implementation of triton.## Possible fix directions(Posted for visibility — happy to author whichever direction the team picks, since I have the empirical setup ready.)1. **`Provides-Dist: triton (==3.X.Y)` in `setup.py`** — makes pip treat `triton-ascend` as fulfilling a `triton` requirement, so xgrammar/vllm/etc. don't pull in mainline triton when triton-ascend is already installed. Easy; non-invasive; right answer if triton-ascend's DSL surface is a strict-enough match for downstream Triton API consumers (which it appears to be, given Ascend's existing user base).2. **`Conflicts: triton` (or PEP 508 advisory)** — refuse to install both. Eliminates ambiguous-state failure but forces users to pick at install time.3. **Namespace packaging** — move triton-ascend's contents under `triton_ascend.backends.ascend` and patch into `triton.backends` at runtime via a Triton entry point hook. Higher engineering cost but no file-level overlap; would survive arbitrary future Triton-mainline `compiler.py` changes.4. **Install-guide doc + post-install hook** — keep the current packaging but warn at install time and provide `triton-ascend-fix-install` shell helper that removes mainline triton's `backends/amd`/`backends/nvidia` directories if present. Least intrusive change but pushes responsibility to users.I'd vote for (1) since it requires only a few lines in `setup.py`, but I understand the team may want (3) for the Triton 3.5 upgrade.## Workaround (verified working today)```bashpip uninstall -y tritonpip install --force-reinstall --no-deps triton-ascend```After this, `import triton` works, `triton.__version__ == 3.2.0`, and `triton.backends.backends == {'ascend': ...}`. Verified on the affected container running: `mindspeed.megatron_adaptor` import OK, miles NPU end-to-end at H=64 SEQ=2048 real DSv4-Flash shape OK, full forward+backward+Adam step OK. xgrammar warns that mainline triton is missing, but its inference-grammar path is not exercised in NPU training workloads.## Note on the repo moveI see the project moved from `gitcode.com/Ascend/triton-ascend` to `github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend` and that gitcode is being archived — I'm filing here at the canonical home. The issue applies to both 3.2.0 and 3.2.1 (just released).Thanks for taking a look.
